title: After-Action Report — Concurrent Saves / Leagues + Restart Data-Loss Fix
scope: The user runs the public O27 site and wanted three things, in their words: the game "is visible for anyone but it doesn't allow you to have a concurrent set of saves or leagues, and it's tough when I can't save the league or access it days later."

rationale.item: Privacy model: Shared named slots — no login. Anyone visiting can list the saved leagues and switch the active one.
Save behaviour: Both — switch between many leagues in-app and export/import a league as a file.
Persistence pain: Data disappears on its own — not just "new league wipes the old," but leagues vanishing between visits.

delegation_events.quote: **Privacy model:** *Shared named slots* — no login. Anyone visiting can list the saved leagues and switch the active one.

delegation_events.decision: Both in-app switching and file export/import
delegation_events.quote: **Save behaviour:** *Both* — switch between many leagues in-app **and** export/import a league as a file.
**No PR opened**, per the user's preference at the time.

actions.description: Multiple named leagues: each league its own SQLite file under a saves directory, tracked by a JSON registry with a single global active pointer; db.get_conn() resolves the active save's file at call time so all ~50 existing routes work unchanged; explicit O27V2_DB_PATH bypasses the registry
/saves page (nav link Saves): list, activate, rename, delete, export, import; active league name in topbar
/new-league no longer wipes the active league — creates a new save file and switches to it
Export via VACUUM INTO (merges WAL, no -wal/-shm sidecars); import validates a teams table before registering
Startup migration: adopts any legacy single DB as Save 1, or creates an initial slot on a fresh box
fly.toml: dropped O27V2_DB_PATH in favour of O27V2_SAVES_DIR=/data/saves

Data-loss fix: _wipe_if_stale() matched every healthy modern league (blank pitcher_role after Task #65); staleness signal now keys on a populated roster with zero archetypes (genuinely pre-Phase-8 data)
New test module and realigned phase8 migration tests

risk_posture: The test-bypass design held. Keying "explicit override = single fixed DB" off O27V2_DB_PATH / _DB_PATH let the existing suite pass untouched, which is what we want from a refactor this broad.

validation_claims.claim: New o27v2/tests/test_saves.py (7 tests) + realigned test_phase8_db_migration.py — all green
Full suite: 179 passed. Remaining failures are pre-existing and confirmed red on main
validation_claims.check_method: Run full pytest suite and compare with main baseline

validation_claims.claim: End-to-end via the Flask test client: create two leagues → both preserved → activate switches the data → export produces a valid single-file DB → import round-trips → rename/delete work → activating no longer wipes
validation_claims.check_method: Repeat the flow via Flask test client

non_actions.item: Deploy step unverified in prod (legacy /data/o27v2.db → Save 1 adoption)
non_actions.reason: tested locally but not on the live volume

non_actions.item: Fixing the two flaky statistical tests
non_actions.reason: Pre-existing; worth seeding or widening tolerances someday
per the user's preference at the time

residual_risks: Deploy step unverified in prod — recommended: deploy, confirm /data/saves/registry.json + save_*.db exist, then restart the machine and confirm the active save survives
Switching saves mid-simulation is guarded against the multi-season runner, but a single long sim request racing an activate is theoretically possible — low risk on a single-user site
Two flaky statistical tests are order/RNG-sensitive (pass standalone, can flake in full-suite runs)
open_hooks: Seed or widen tolerances on the flaky statistical tests
Prod verification checklist for the Save 1 adoption
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Concurrent Saves / Leagues + Restart Data-Loss Fix
unmapped_content: Header metadata: Branch claude/concurrent-saves-leagues-q6DrT; commit 417eb63. What went well: the architecture made the refactor cheap (get_conn() opens a fresh connection with no pooling/caching — ~15-line change in one place); verification caught the real bug (running an actual /new-league → activate flow surfaced the wipe-on-restart; a green unit suite alone would have shipped the latent bug). What surprised us: the root cause wasn't the feature; a destructive heuristic ran unconditionally on a hot path (auto drop_all() at startup is a footgun); tests encoded a dead data model (test_phase8 failures already red on main asserted pitcher_role == 'workhorse' and is_joker rows that no longer exist). What was harder than expected: telling 'my regression' from 'already broken' needed git stash baseline runs (three failures were pre-existing); fresh-install edge case would have seeded into the unregistered fallback file — fixed by creating the slot before seeding. Lessons: (1) Config looking correct ≠ data being safe. (2) A stale heuristic is created by a change elsewhere — Task #65 broke _wipe_if_stale from a distance. (3) Verify behaviour, not just the suite. (4) Tests that drift become camouflage. Restart trigger detail: each Fly restart (redeploy, 512 MB OOM, idle cycle) silently dropped the league and reseeded a fresh one.
